summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service by acknowledging the reality of spiritual warfare and the opposition faced by the church, but emphasized the greater power of Jesus Christ and the unbreakable love of God. He then transitioned into the second part of his series on love, focusing on 1 Corinthians 13. The central message was that true, God-like love is selfless. Pastor Travis explained that selfishness is a root cause of conflict, broken relationships, and personal emptiness, contrasting it with the selfless nature of God's love demonstrated through Jesus. He taught that selflessness is cultivated through three key components: total surrender to God, a servant's heart that sees and meets needs, and a willingness to make sacrificial giving a lifestyle. The sermon contrasted the 'Mary spirit' of generous sacrifice with the 'Judas spirit' of selfish greed, urging listeners to examine whether they are givers or takers. Pastor Travis concluded with a powerful call to receive God's forgiveness and to extend that same forgiveness to others, as a selfless act that leads to healing and wholeness. He invited those present to surrender their lives to Christ.
mainPoints: True, God-like love is fundamentally selfless, putting others first and countering our natural selfish, narcissistic tendencies.
A selfless life is built on three pillars: total surrender to God, a servant's heart that proactively sees needs, and a willingness to make sacrifices.
We must choose between a generous 'Mary spirit' that gives sacrificially and a greedy 'Judas spirit' that takes, and we must forgive others as God has forgiven us.

versesMentioned.text: No one is like you, LORD; you are great, and your name is mighty in power.

For I am convinced that neither death nor life, neither angels nor demons, neither the present nor the future, nor any powers, neither height nor depth, nor anything else in all creation, will be able to separate us from the love of God that is in Christ Jesus our Lord.

Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud. It does not dishonor others, it is not self-seeking, it is not easily angered, it keeps no record of wrongs. Love does not delight in evil but rejoices with the truth. It always protects, always trusts, always hopes, always perseveres. Love never fails.

But mark this: There will be terrible times in the last days. People will be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boastful, proud, abusive, disobedient to their parents, ungrateful, unholy, without love, unforgiving, slanderous, without self-control, brutal, not lovers of the good, treacherous, rash, conceited, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God— having a form of godliness but denying its power. Have nothing to do with such people.
Do nothing out of selfish ambition or vain conceit. Rather, in humility value others above yourselves, not looking to your own interests but each of you to the interests of the others.
For even the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.
Greater love has no one than this: to lay down one’s life for one’s friends.
Therefore, I urge you, brothers and sisters, in view of God’s mercy, to offer your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to God—this is your true and proper worship.

Here a dinner was given in Jesus’ honor. Martha served, while Lazarus was among those reclining at the table with him. Then Mary took about a pint of pure nard, an expensive perfume; she poured it on Jesus’ feet and wiped his feet with her hair. And the house was filled with the fragrance of the perfume. But one of his disciples, Judas Iscariot, who was later to betray him, objected, “Why wasn’t this perfume sold and the money given to the poor? It was worth a year’s wages.” He did not say this because he cared about the poor but because he was a thief; as keeper of the money bag, he used to help himself to what was put into it.
If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just and will forgive us our sins and purify us from all unrighteousness.
Jesus said, “Father, forgive them, for they do not know what they are doing.” And they divided up his clothes by casting lots.

Monday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 13:5 - "It does not dishonor others, it is not self-seeking, it is not easily angered, it keeps no record of wrongs."
Monday.action: Identify one relationship today where you tend to be self-focused. Make a conscious choice to ask about the other person's needs and defer your own preference once.
Monday.prayer: God, help me to see where I make things about me. Give me a selfless heart that seeks to put others first, starting today.

Tuesday.scripture: Philippians 2:3-4 - "Do nothing out of selfish ambition... in humility value others above yourselves."
Tuesday.focus: The call to humility and valuing others.
Tuesday.action: Perform a simple, unnoticed act of service for a family member or coworker without expecting recognition or thanks.
Tuesday.prayer: Lord, train my heart to serve humbly. Let my joy come from blessing others, not from being noticed.

Wednesday.scripture: Mark 10:45 - "For even the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve."
Wednesday.action: Practice 'seeing' needs. At home or work, look for one practical need you can meet without being asked, and do it.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, give me your eyes to see the needs around me. Make me proactive in serving, just as you were.

Thursday.scripture: Romans 12:1 - "Offer your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to God."
Thursday.action: Make a financial gift that genuinely requires a sacrifice, not just leftover funds. Let it be an act of worship.
Thursday.prayer: Father, I surrender my resources to you. Teach me the joy of sacrificial giving, trusting that you are my true provider.

Friday.scripture: 1 John 1:9 - "If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just and will forgive us."
Friday.action: Spend time in confession, writing down anything for which you need God's forgiveness. Then, thank Him for His promise to forgive and purify.
Friday.prayer: Thank you, God, for your complete forgiveness. I receive your cleansing and release the guilt I have carried.

Saturday.scripture: Luke 23:34 - "Jesus said, 'Father, forgive them, for they do not know what they are doing.'"
Saturday.action: Choose one person you need to forgive. Write a letter of forgiveness to them (you don't have to send it). Release the record of wrongs to God.
Saturday.prayer: Holy Spirit, this is hard. I choose to forgive [name] as you have forgiven me. Heal my heart and set me free from bitterness.
